sections.paragraph:
ὀπτός, ή, όν (Hom. et al.; pap; Ex 12:8, 9; Jos., Ant. 3, 255) pert. to preparation of food by direct fire (opp. ἕψω boil), roasted, baked, broiled ἰχθὺς ὀπ. broiled fish (Hippocr., Aff. 52 vol. VI p. 264 L. ἰχθύες ὀπτοί; Plut., Mor. 353d; PCairZen 66, 13; 16 [III b.c.]; PGiss 93, 6 [II a.d.]) Lk 24:42.—S. the lit. s.v. μελίσσιος.—DELG. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

  1. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 717
